steps.title: Set up the Pythagorean theorem
steps.content:
A right triangle has one leg of 37.8 mm and a hypotenuse of 172.2 mm. Using the Pythagorean theorem, where $a = 37.8$, $c = 172.2$, and $b$ is the unknown leg:
$$37.8^2 + b^2 = 172.2^2$$

steps.title: Calculate and solve for the other leg
steps.content:
Compute both squares:
$$1428.84 + b^2 = 29652.84$$
Subtract 1428.84 from both sides:
$$b^2 = 28224$$
Take the positive square root:
$$b = \sqrt{28224} = 168$$
That matches choice C — C is correct.
